conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do! I have two children - Alex who is 6 years old, and Blake who is 3 years old. They both live with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's good to know you have two children, Alex (6 years old) and Blake (3 years old), who both live with you.

To continue assessing your eligibility, I need to ask: Are you currently living in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great. Now I need to ask about your relationship to the children.

Are you the parent of Alex and Blake, or do you have another relationship to them (such as being their guardian, foster carer, or another type of carer)?

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Now I need to check a few more things to assess your eligibility properly.

Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for either Alex or Blake?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, no one else is claiming Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment.

Are either Alex or Blake currently in hospital or in care (such as local authority care)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, Blake has been in hospital for a few months now - about 3 months or so.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I need to understand this situation a bit better.

Has Blake been in hospital continuously for the entire 3 months, and do you know if Blake is expected to remain in hospital for much longer?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Blake has been in hospital continuously for the whole 3 months. I'm not entirely sure how much longer they'll need to stay.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I see. This is an important detail. One more question about Blake's hospital stay:

During Blake's time in hospital, have you been contributing to the cost of Blake's maintenance or care (for example, visiting regularly, providing clothes, or other support)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Unfortunately, I haven't been able to afford to spend money on their needs while they're in there.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for being honest about your situation. I have one final question to complete my assessment:

During Blake's hospital stay, have you been visiting Blake regularly or maintaining contact with them?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I have been keeping in touch, but in terms of actually spending money on things they need - clothes, toys, treats, that sort of thing - I just haven't been able to manage it financially.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for providing all that information. Let me assess your eligibility for Child Benefit based on what you've told me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on my assessment, here's what I've determined about your Child Benefit eligibility:

**For Alex:** ✅ **You are eligible**
Alex is 6 years old, lives with you in the UK, you are their parent, and no one else is claiming for them. You meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.

**For Blake:** ❌ **You are not currently eligible**
While Blake is 3 years old and you are their parent, Blake has been in hospital continuously for approximately 3 months (12 weeks). According to UK Child Benefit rules, you can continue to claim Child Benefit for a child in hospital for up to 12 weeks, but only if you are contributing to the cost of their maintenance. Since you've indicated that you haven't been able to afford to spend money on Blake's needs while they're in hospital, the eligibility for Blake would have ended after the 12-week period.

**What this means:** You can claim or continue to claim Child Benefit for Alex, but your eligibility for Blake has unfortunately ceased due to the length of their hospital stay and the maintenance contribution requirement.

If Blake's situation changes or they come home from hospital, you would be able to claim Child Benefit for them again at that time.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 6 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 3 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 6 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 3 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child in hospital/residential accommodation for 13 weeks (>12) and claimant is not regularly spending money on child
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex

Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 6 years old (under 16), lives with you in the UK, you are their parent, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for them. Alex is not in hospital or care. You meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake is 3 years old (under 16) and you are their parent living in the UK, with no one else claiming for them. However, Blake has been in hospital continuously for 3 months (12 weeks). According to UK Child Benefit rules, you can continue to claim Child Benefit for a child in hospital for up to 12 weeks (84 days), but only if you are contributing to the cost of their maintenance. Since you have stated that you have not been able to afford to spend money on Blake's needs while in hospital, you would not meet the requirement of contributing to their maintenance. Therefore, Child Benefit eligibility for Blake would cease after the 12-week period has ended.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a parent of two children (Alex, 6, and Blake, 3) living in the UK with no one else claiming for them. Alex lives at home and meets all eligibility criteria. Blake has been in hospital continuously for approximately 3 months. Under Child Benefit rules, eligibility continues for children in hospital for up to 12 weeks if the claimant contributes to the child's maintenance. Since the claimant has not been able to contribute financially to Blake's maintenance and the 12-week period has passed, eligibility for Blake would not continue beyond that period. The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex but not for Blake.
